meta.name: Electra Frost
meta.tagline: The open, machine-readable intellectual provenance of Electra Frost
meta.description: A specialist public accountant who has been reinventing professional practice at the digital frontier for 25 years - before it was called that. This is her aggregated, sovereign and discoverable record of ideas, contributions and predictions: how the thinking developed, where it came from, and where it is going.

eras.label: Creative Industries
eras.description: Came into accounting from the music industry in 1998 to help musicians not fear tax. Built Electra Frost Advisory around creative industries — film, music, arts. CTA Fellow 2007, CTA 2011. The philosophical root: accountants as trusted intermediaries who make complex systems navigable for people who'd otherwise be excluded.

eras.label: Bitcoin Conviction
eras.description: Discovered Bitcoin in 2013 as a film-maker's accountant. First public posts 2019 on automation and professional value. The seed question: what does the accountant's role look like when money itself becomes programmable?

eras.label: Accountants OnChain
eras.description: RMIT blockchain study (started 2018), Blockchain Collective Advanced Diploma (2022). Founded Accountants OnChain with Digital Playhouse Foundation — Australia's first community of crypto-curious accountants. The conviction: crypto-literate small business accountants are the missing layer in mass adoption.

eras.label: ElectraFi Practice
eras.description: Pivoted 15-year-old Electra Frost Advisory into ElectraFi — specialist crypto accounting. Built crypto chart of accounts, subledger software stacks, monthly reconciliation workflows for crypto-native businesses. Proof by doing: built the model before it existed.

eras.label: Bitcoin & ESG
eras.description: While everyone attacked Bitcoin on the 'E' (energy), pointed to the S and G — proof-of-work as socially ethical, Bitcoin bootstrapping renewable energy in developing countries, grid stability, energy sovereignty for regions that have none.

eras.label: CREDU & Credentials
eras.description: POAP as proof of CPD attendance — demoed at AB Expo. CREDU as a decentralised professional association. Members own and monetise their knowledge, blockchain-verified credentials. The accounting body monopoly on credentialling is the next thing to break.

eras.label: Bitcoin Standard
eras.description: Bitcoin as unit of account — not payment, not store of value alone — but the base measurement standard for a new economy. Composite Fractionalized Money, sBTC on Stacks, dynamic payment design for subscription states. Lessons from Próspera as first startup society on Bitcoin UoA.

eras.label: Network State & d/acc
eras.description: Token2049 Singapore → Network School Cohort 1 (90 days). Living the thesis: departed Australian home, building at Forest City island SEZ near Singapore. Writing web3 accounting and digital nomad tax classes for Network Schoolers. 'I'm the only accountant here and I want to change that.'

eras.label: Tax & Professional Bodies
eras.description: 25 years tax representation — ATO most active ever. SMSF Bitcoin advice. AML/CTF Tranche 2 reform. Tax Institute/IPA amalgamation — strong support as a federated model analogous to DAO structure. The tax practitioner as frontline professional caught between innovation and regulatory overreach.

eras.label: CPD PeerLab
eras.description: Presented to IPA WA August 2025 — a member-led CPD marketplace without blockchain, using IPA's existing infrastructure. Members as creators not consumers. The pre-blockchain MVP of CREDU, stress-tested inside a legacy institution. 'In the age of Gen-AI, credible learning is live, peer-led, and demonstrated.'

eras.label: AI Governance
eras.description: BlueDot Impact: AGI Strategy (30hr), then Frontier AI Governance (first cohort). Kill chain analysis, threat models, defence-in-depth. Core thesis: the public accounting profession, obligated to the public interest through IFAC standards across 130 jurisdictions, is structurally suited to provide independent AI governance over AI labs and governments alike.

eras.label: AI in Accounting
eras.description: Python script for tax substantiation → LLM → OneDrive, built during a conference session. Vendor CPD conflict of interest: sponsors want product awareness, not practitioner independence. CREDU Academy 'accountants hackerspace' at Network School. 'Give an accountant a SaaS subscription and you transform their practice for a year. Teach an accountant to code with AI and you transform it for life.'

eras.label: Network Residency
eras.description: Position paper: network societies will force the emergence of a non-geographic transnational resident class whose tax treatment is negotiated collectively through the network-state entity. Status, not zone. V1→V5→Beyond taxonomy. Goes beyond both Balaji's territory-first thesis and Próspera's zone-bound model.

eras.label: AI Safety
eras.description: AI safety as a social and governance (S+G) imperative. Public accountants as the missing AI governance intermediary. Making AI-intensive  reporting safe, contestable and investment-grade. Robodebt as the crystallising case: what happens when no one is minding the automated system.

nodes.title: Code Is Not Law — Separation of Powers in the Agentic Economy
nodes.subtitle: Why 'code is law' cannot hold a network society together
nodes.body: Published to Substack April 2026. Code is execution. It performs an executive function. The legislative function — deciding what the rules are — and the judicial function — interpreting those rules when facts are ambiguous or contested — are different tasks. Any system that exercises authority over people, assets or decisions needs all three powers separated. Accountants already operate across all three layers.

nodes.title: The Public Interest Intermediary AI Governance is Missing
nodes.subtitle: IFAC-bound accountants as cross-border AI governance layer
nodes.body: Published on Substack March 2026. Most AI systems proposals assume trusted intermediaries exist — they depend on labs to self-report, regulators to keep pace, or new oversight bodies to emerge in time. Nobody explains where those intermediaries come from or what binds them. The IFAC Code of Conduct binds 3 million professionals across 130 jurisdictions to act in the public interest. That obligation is enforceable, independent, carries consequences and works across borders. Global Public Accountants are the cross-border intermediary layer that currently has no station on the AI governance map.

nodes.title: IPA + Tax Institute Amalgamation — Federation, Not Merger
nodes.subtitle: The federated model is structurally closer to a DAO
nodes.body: Attended the Tax Institute member consultation in Perth. Strongly supports the amalgamation proposal — but as federation, not merger. An amalgamation model is structurally much closer to the direction accounting needs to go — shared infrastructure, maintained professional identity. Analogous to the DAO structure being proposed for decentralised professional association.

nodes.title: Tax Law Is Not a Lookup Table
nodes.subtitle: What an AI tax tool exposed inside a startup society
nodes.body: Published to Substack March 2026. Tax statutes are written as deterministic rules. It is easy to assume code is law. The difficulty is that professional tax work rarely begins with reliable inputs. Facts are incomplete. Client records are contradictory. Stated intentions conflict with transaction patterns. An AI tool can assist with research and drafting. It cannot replace interpretive judgment or carry the professional obligation.

nodes.title: ATO Compliance Surge
nodes.subtitle: 25 years — a never seen it this active
nodes.body: 'In 25 years of tax representation, I haven't seen the Australian Tax Office this active. Taking professional advice upfront is now business-critical. Accountants don't have capacity for clean-ups and rescue jobs anymore.' The profession is under pressure from both directions: AI threatening compliance revenue, and regulators increasing enforcement. The accountant's independent public interest role has never been more needed.

nodes.title: BlueDot Impact — Frontier AI Governance
nodes.subtitle: First cohort: the trust gap and the missing intermediary
nodes.body: Completed BlueDot Impact's Frontier AI Governance course — first cohort, selective intake. Focused on the trust gap: most AI governance proposals assume reliable non-conflicted intermediaries exist. None explain where they come from or what enforceable obligation binds them. Action plan: global public accountants as a cross-border intermediary layer — mandated by an independent public-interest obligation that currently has no clear place in AI governance.

nodes.title: Bitcoin Cannot Be Regulated and Doesn't Need to Be
nodes.subtitle: The regulatory framework for everything adjacent to Bitcoin
nodes.body: Bitcoin cannot be regulated and does not need to be. That is the starting point. Everything adjacent to Bitcoin needs regulation to the extent that it departs from Bitcoin's security model. The further from Bitcoin's base layer, the more regulatory risk exists — because counterparties and trust assumptions increase. Accountants are the natural layer to provide that regulation, not governments.

nodes.title: CREDU Academy at Network School
nodes.subtitle: The accountants' hackerhouse — building the new profession
nodes.body: Establishing CREDU Academy at Network School — an accounting technologist hackerspace where practitioners learn to build their own tools, verify credentials on-chain, and design compliance systems for agentic economies. Give an accountant a SaaS subscription and you transform their practice for a year. Teach an accountant to code with AI and you transform their practice for life.

nodes.title: From Tax Residency to Network Residency
nodes.subtitle: The pathway to diplomatic recognition through tax status
nodes.body: Position paper: network societies will force the emergence of a non-geographic tax residency framework. V1: full compliance with existing domestic laws. V2: treaty-based carve-outs. V3: bilateral recognition agreements. V4: multinational frameworks. V5: diplomatic recognition of network residency as a legal status. Origin: a 2012 international tax masters paper proposing a global tax system for global citizens. The idea predated Network States by over a decade.

nodes.title: Freo Neuhaus
nodes.subtitle: Seeding a startup society node in Fremantle, Western Australia
nodes.body: Freo Neuhaus: seeding a node of the Network School global network in Fremantle, WA. A port city with a history of radicalism, creative industries, and openness to experiment. A network society without professional services infrastructure is a society that cannot account for itself. The accounting and professional services component is foundational, not administrative.

nodes.title: Bitcoin Is Accounting. Accountants Are Bitcoin.
nodes.subtitle: The most explicit statement of the convergence thesis
nodes.body: September 2025: Accounting, at its core, is about truth in value, ethics, integrity of records, and the protection of purchasing power over time. Bitcoin matters because it reintroduces objective scarcity. It is a programmable accounting primitive on an immutable, time-stamped ledger. The hashtags BitcoinIsAccounting and AccountantsAreBitcoin as position statements. The accountants who study this now will be the ones designing the future of our role.

nodes.title: CPD PeerLab
nodes.subtitle: Peer-led CPD marketplace — presented to IPA WA
nodes.body: Presented to IPA WA August 2025: a member-led CPD marketplace where practitioners deliver and spend CPD credits or charge a fair fee. IPA retains a facilitation share. Supported by AI tools. WA and Malaysia cohort. In the age of Gen-AI, credible learning is live, peer-led, and demonstrated. The proto-CREDU model in practice.

nodes.title: Bitcoin Is Not a Payments Platform
nodes.subtitle: Disagreeing with Jack Dorsey — fundamentally
nodes.body: People keep talking about using Bitcoin for payments. This idea is holding us back. Jack Dorsey said Bitcoin must be payments for it to be relevant. Disagrees fundamentally. Bitcoin is the world's number one ledger — the most secure settlement layer for ALL digital currencies and financial instruments on ALL chains. BTC-for-payments is not essential — what matters is that everything settles back to Bitcoin's fixed supply.

nodes.title: GovFi — Decentralised Model for Government Efficiency
nodes.subtitle: Crowdsourcing government functions to verified citizens
nodes.body: GovFi: a decentralised model where government functions are crowdsourced to citizens and rewarded for verified contributions, reducing bureaucracy and increasing accountability. The accounting profession — with its existing public interest mandate, global federation, and verification infrastructure — is the natural operator of this model. Governments cannot self-govern; accountants can provide the independent layer.

nodes.title: Bitcoin as Unit of Account
nodes.subtitle: Not payment, not store of value — the base measurement standard
nodes.body: Most see Bitcoin as a Store of Value or Means of Exchange. Its pivotal function as a Unit of Account (UoA) is less explored. A UoA is essential for measuring and comparing economic value — much like the US dollar in pricing and financial reporting. For global 'cloud countries,' adopting Bitcoin as UoA represents a logical shift away from reliance on the USD. Presented research at Network School on Composite Fractionalized Money using sBTC (Stacks) and stablecoins.

nodes.title: Decentralised Professional Association — The Call
nodes.subtitle: Member-driven, digital, globally scaled, distributed governance
nodes.body: November 2024: Imagine a decentralised professional association — member-driven, digital, globally scaled and collaborative, low-cost, and with distributed governance and ownership by us. The clearest early public articulation of the DPA concept beyond DAO theory. Asking who would join a research group to explore it. The response was strongly positive.

nodes.title: Nomadic Tax Compliance — The Gap No One is Filling
nodes.subtitle: AI tax tools at Network School are dangerously wrong
nodes.body: An AI tax tool circulating inside Network School was giving dangerously wrong advice on Malaysian tax residency. Pivoted to designing a scalable model for defensible, global tax compliance for nomadic crypto netizens — logic-driven, auditable, with qualified human judgment at every step. We can build a proper system: structured by professionals, logic-driven, auditable, and up to legal standards. The professional layer that startup societies are missing.

nodes.title: Network School Cohort 1 — Forest City, Malaysia
nodes.subtitle: Living the thesis in a startup society
nodes.body: September 2024: selected into V1 first cohort of Network School, Forest City, Malaysia — a man-made island facing Singapore. The only accountant there, learning and building with purpose. The experiment of living inside a startup society while building the professional infrastructure it needs. Every conversation confirmed the thesis: network societies need accountants more than they realise.

nodes.title: Quadruple-Entry Accounting
nodes.subtitle: AI generates claims; crypto verifies them; accountants validate the proofs
nodes.body: Are you ready for Quadruple-Entry Accounting? AI is going to create lots of jobs in verification — taking on-chain proof to off-chain. Beyond blockchain triple-entry, quadruple-entry becomes the accounting industry backbone of attestable computation and verifiable financial outcomes. AI generates claims; crypto verifies them; accountants validate the proofs across systems.

nodes.title: AML/CTF Tranche 2 — Time to Step Back
nodes.subtitle: The regulatory risk is now too high for traditional facilitation services
nodes.body: With Tranche 2 reforms coming, re-evaluating services that fall into AML/CTF territory: entity setup, acting as ASIC agent, offshore structuring, assisting with crypto accounts. Never been fully comfortable. Planning to go hands-off. A position that accelerated the pivot toward advisory-only and protocol-level work. The profession needs better infrastructure, not fewer practitioners.

nodes.title: DAO vs Merger — Merge the Accountants, Not Their Bodies
nodes.subtitle: If we're creating a monopoly, make it a decentralised one
nodes.body: Published to Substack December 2023. A proposed merger of Australia's accounting bodies signals further centralisation — the wrong direction. A DAO would merge the members, not the institutions. Rather than forming a dominant entity, a DAO would merge the members of these bodies into a member-governed digital organisation. The professional bodies remain; the members gain sovereignty.

nodes.title: CREDU
nodes.subtitle: Global decentralised CPD/CPE marketplace
nodes.body: CREDU is a global, peer-led CPE/CPD marketplace and public dashboard that uses blockchain to verify expertise. Four user journeys: accountants, CPE trainers, professional bodies, and the public. Members own and monetise their knowledge. IPA hosts and accredits. Strengthens IPA's credibility and member competitiveness. Pitched for Ready Layer 2 Bitcoin Builders competition at WAWeb3 2024.

nodes.title: Stacks Australia
nodes.subtitle: Bitcoin Layer 2 developer community
nodes.body: Co-founded Stacks Australia with Gordon Christian — a special purpose collective to support founders, developers, investors, creators and community managers building on Layer 2 Bitcoin. Ran Nakamoto Launch events across Brisbane, organised workshops, connected the Bitcoin builders movement in Australia. Stacks makes Bitcoin programmable, enabling decentralized apps and smart contracts that inherit Bitcoin's security.

nodes.title: POAP as Proof of CPD
nodes.subtitle: Demoed at AB Expo — blockchain-native credentials
nodes.body: At Accounting & Business Expo 2023, ran a live demonstration of POAP (Proof of Attendance Protocol) as blockchain-native CPD logging. Onboarded accountants to non-custodial wallets, minted their first NFTs, showed them why CPD logging on blockchain makes sense. 'If only there was an EASIER way to log my CPD in REAL TIME that doesn't cost me, is owned by me, and is transparent and verifies my know-how to anyone who wants to see it, anytime.' This was the seed of CREDU.

nodes.title: IPA National Conference — Getting Raw About Bitcoin
nodes.subtitle: The Xero analogy that landed for the mainstream profession
nodes.body: Presented at IPA National Conference, Noosa 2023. Some people say they have no interest in Bitcoin but the technology behind it sounds useful. As a futurist accountant, that is like hearing: I would like to use Xero but do not need to learn accounting. The analogy that made the mainstream profession stop and think.

nodes.title: Anti-Crypto is Anti-Competitive
nodes.subtitle: Banks discriminating against crypto businesses violate competition principles
nodes.body: Anti-crypto is anti-competitive. Competition leads to lower prices, more choice and better products for consumers. Those innovating with crypto and blockchain use cases are driving greater value and protections to us than ever before, financially and personally. Time to challenge all banks and corporations that discriminate against crypto businesses. A legal and moral argument, not just an ideological one.

nodes.title: Accountants DAO on Stacks — First Articulation
nodes.subtitle: We can build Accountants DAO on Stacks protocol
nodes.body: January 2023: We can build Accountants DAO on Stacks protocol. The first explicit naming of a DAO architecture for the accounting profession. Not a reform of existing bodies — a parallel, member-governed structure built on open protocols. This would evolve into CREDU and the decentralised professional association thesis.

nodes.title: Bitcoin is an ESG Asset
nodes.subtitle: S and G matter as much as E
nodes.body: While critics focused on Bitcoin's energy use, the social and governance dimensions were being overlooked. Proof-of-work is socially ethical. Bitcoin bootstraps sustainable energy infrastructure in developing countries. It enables energy sovereignty. 'People focusing heavily on the environmental E side of ESG as it relates to Bitcoin often overlook the SG component — at the end of the day, Bitcoin is one of the most ESG assets around.' Planning ESG reporting services for Bitcoin businesses from 2023.

nodes.title: Bitcoin is an ESG Asset — S and G Matter as Much as E
nodes.subtitle: The SG component is consistently overlooked
nodes.body: People focusing heavily on the environmental E side of ESG as it relates to Bitcoin often overlook the SG component — at the end of the day, Bitcoin is one of the most ESG assets around. Proof-of-work is socially ethical: Bitcoin bootstraps sustainable energy, enables energy sovereignty, and provides the governance transparency that traditional finance lacks.

nodes.title: Proof of State
nodes.subtitle: Blockchain-verified financial statements
nodes.body: Seven years after falling down the Ethereum rabbit hole following a regulatory conflict related to an accounting treatment, articulated the concept of Proof of State: the validation of 100% of all accounting information from transactions to financial statements using Merkle trees and a blockchain. This term was derived from the idea of a state machine. Engaged with Auditchain Labs on the protocol.

nodes.title: ElectraFi
nodes.subtitle: Specialist crypto accounting practice
nodes.body: Pivoted 15-year-old Electra Frost Advisory into ElectraFi ℔ specialist crypto accounting for businesses operating in digital assets. Built crypto chart of accounts, subledger software stacks (Cryptoworth, AEM Journaler), monthly reconciliation workflows. Exhibited at AB Expo 2023, spoke at Xerocon. Trained Lisa Wilson as senior crypto accountant. Four day working week with Friday as tech/PD day.

nodes.title: Xerocon 2022 — Blockchain Panel
nodes.subtitle: Urging Xero accountants to read the Bitcoin whitepaper
nodes.body: Spoke at Xerocon Sydney 2022, urging a packed house of crypto-curious Xero accountants to read the Bitcoin whitepaper, learn by doing, and be pioneers of best practice for Web3 business. Accountants onboarded everyone to cloud accounting and we can do this. The moment the mainstream accounting software community started listening.

nodes.title: Triple Entry Accounting — The Blockchain Accounting Primitive
nodes.subtitle: The accounting implications of an immutable public ledger
nodes.body: Attending the Enterprise Blockchain Conference 2022, hearing about triple entry accounting — blockchain adds a third entry (the cryptographic receipt on a shared ledger) to double-entry bookkeeping. The spark: if triple entry is where we are, quadruple entry is where we're going. AI generates claims; crypto verifies them; accountants validate the proofs.

nodes.title: Accountants OnChain
nodes.subtitle: Australia's first crypto-curious accountants community
nodes.body: Founded Accountants OnChain with Digital Playhouse Foundation — a community of small business accountants learning crypto, blockchain and Web3 together. Group tickets to Australian Crypto Convention paid in crypto, received as NFTs. The conviction: crypto-literate small business accountants are the missing layer in mass adoption. They onboarded Australia to cloud accounting — they can do this too.

nodes.title: Auditchain — Automated Accountancy on Blockchain
nodes.subtitle: Seeing the future of audit and financial reporting
nodes.body: Discovered Auditchain — the world's first decentralised continuous audit and reporting protocol. Hosted Australian meetups with Andrew Noble to explore process-control NFTs, XBRL machine-readable reports, and proof-of-state validation. Blockchain, cryptography and Bitcoin PoW provide the underlying internet and financial infrastructure for evidence-grade records. The earliest articulation of accountants as infrastructure, not just compliance.

nodes.title: Stacks Australia
nodes.subtitle: Bitcoin Layer 2 chapter — building on Bitcoin
nodes.body: Digital Playhouse Foundation confirmed as recipient of the Stacks ANZ Chapter Grant. Stacks Australia launched — supporting founders, developers, investors and creators building on Layer 2 Bitcoin. The conviction: Bitcoin's programmable layers would be where the accounting profession's future work was conducted.

nodes.title: Taking Wages in Bitcoin
nodes.subtitle: Practising what I preach — DCA through payroll
nodes.body: Started taking wages in Bitcoin. 'I take wages in Bitcoin. So does the Mayor of New York! Easy dollar cost averaging.' This was not theoretical — it required navigating the actual Australian tax and payroll law complexities: PAYG, salary sacrifice agreements, STP reporting, FIFO inventory treatment for Bitcoin coming into and out of the business.

nodes.title: CBDCs Are a Threat to Financial Sovereignty
nodes.subtitle: Any accountant promoting themselves as a small business advocate must study this
nodes.body: Any accountant promoting themselves as a small business advocate should be objectively apprehending the societal consequences of a retail CBDC and studying Bitcoin and decentralised blockchain technologies now. Their clients will. CBDCs represent programmable surveillance money — the antithesis of the permissionless, trust-minimised financial infrastructure Bitcoin enables.

nodes.title: Taking Wages in Bitcoin
nodes.subtitle: Practising what is preached
nodes.body: Now only offering advisory services for payment in BTC with part of salary paid in BTC. Dollar cost averaging through earned income, not just buying. Living the Bitcoin standard professionally before advocating it to others. A demonstration that practitioners can operate in a Bitcoin-denominated way within existing legal and tax frameworks.

nodes.title: Accountants OnChain
nodes.subtitle: Australia's first community of crypto-curious accountants
nodes.body: Launched Accountants OnChain under Digital Playhouse Foundation — webinars, training, consulting and speaking for accountants, bookkeepers and advisers learning to support Web3 businesses. Crypto subledger tools, DAO research, blockchain accounting projects, tax ethics, peer problem-solving. The conviction: accountants needed to learn this now, not wait for regulators to catch up.

nodes.title: Centralised vs Decentralised — The Core Distinction
nodes.subtitle: First articulation of the open protocols argument
nodes.body: June 2021: Financial literacy in the digital age begins with being able to distinguish between centralised and decentralised purpose. The innovations in identity and data are being driven by blockchain — permissionless, trustless, decentralised identity systems — in contrast to centralised authority models. This distinction became the foundational frame for everything that followed.

nodes.title: Digital Playhouse Foundation
nodes.subtitle: Public Benevolent Institution for Bitcoin education
nodes.body: Registered Digital Playhouse Foundation as a Public Benevolent Institution in 2021 with Gordon Christian to 'advance public and social welfare' through Bitcoin-powered digital and financial literacy. Projects include The School of Bitcoin and Stacks Australia. Accepted into El Salvador's My First Bitcoin Node Network in 2024.

nodes.title: Mental Health First Aid Certificate
nodes.subtitle: Financial distress and mental health are linked
nodes.body: Obtained MHFA Certificate from Mental Health First Aid International. There is a clear link between financial distress and mental health issues — being a trusted adviser requires mental health literacy as well as accounting skills. Ran MusicKeeper and CrewKeeper crisis relief grants for music industry workers during COVID.

nodes.title: The Automation Question
nodes.subtitle: Can this person add value beyond what a robot can do?
nodes.body: First public LinkedIn posts in 2019 quoted Thomas Friedman: 'Can this person add value every hour, every day — more than a worker in India, a robot or a computer? Can he or she help my company adapt by not only doing the job today but also reinventing the job for tomorrow?' This frame — the accountant as irreplaceable human intermediary in an automated world — became the root of everything that followed.

nodes.title: Agnes Water — Australia's First Digital Currency Town
nodes.subtitle: Building Bitcoin literacy in regional Queensland
nodes.body: Agnes Water, where Electra was based, was named Australia's first Digital Currency Town in 2018. Operating a remote accounting practice from regional Queensland while Bitcoin and blockchain were still fringe topics in the profession. The isolation was generative — space to think differently about what money, value, and professional services could become.

nodes.title: RMIT Blockchain Study
nodes.subtitle: Started blockchain education in 2018
nodes.body: Started studying blockchain at RMIT in 2018, followed by Blockchain Collective Advanced Diploma in 2022. 'Learning blockchain and trying to explain it to accountants is like being back where I was 20 years ago... studying tax law and trying to explain it to artists.' Through competence we overcome fear — and through shared and relatable knowledge, a positive difference is made to many lives.

nodes.title: RMIT Blockchain Strategy
nodes.subtitle: First formal blockchain study
nodes.body: Completed RMIT University's Developing Blockchain Strategy course in 2018 — conceptual understanding of blockchain technology, use cases across industries, and strategic frameworks. The same year Agnes Water was named Australia's first Digital Currency Town. The professional conviction was forming: this technology was about accounting and governance, not just speculation.

nodes.title: Bitcoin Discovery
nodes.subtitle: Found Bitcoin in 2013 as a film-maker's accountant
nodes.body: Discovered Bitcoin in 2013 as a film-maker's accountant. The conviction formed early: this was not a passing technology. Artists and accountants can lead us to the Bitcoin Standard. The insight that money itself becoming programmable changes the accountant's role — not eliminates it, changes it — would drive the next decade of work.

nodes.title: Bitcoin on the Balance Sheet
nodes.subtitle: First encounter: a film-maker's accountant pays overseas artists in BTC
nodes.body: Discovered Bitcoin in 2013 through a film production client paying overseas artists in BTC. In the absence of official guidance, applied FOREX accounting principles and built the first spreadsheets. The conviction formed immediately: this was not a passing technology. 'We first put Bitcoin on the balance sheet in 2014.' The start of everything.

nodes.title: Fellow of the Tax Institute
nodes.subtitle: CTA attained 2011 — pinacle of tax profession
nodes.body: Became a Fellow of the Tax Institute in 2007, later attaining Chartered Tax Adviser designation in 2011. Completed two tax degrees. The CTA is the global mark of expertise in taxation. Combined with FIPA from the Institute of Public Accountants, creates a unique dual professional mandate.

nodes.title: Electra Frost Advisory
nodes.subtitle: 15-year accounting practice for creatives and SMEs
nodes.body: Built Electra Frost Advisory from Sydney, then moved fully online to regional Queensland (Agnes Water). Specialised in creative industries and SMEs. Built a team, moved to cloud accounting before most, navigated clients through the pandemic. Practice became the laboratory for everything that followed: proof that an accountant could build, adapt, and lead rather than just comply.

nodes.title: entArt Accounting — Music Industry Accounting
nodes.subtitle: The original creative industries practice
nodes.body: Joined entArt Accounting, Bondi Junction — specialist business management and tax services for the music industry, established by Barry Moore CA. The formative experience: accounting as a service to people who create, not just people who comply. Spent nearly 6 years here before founding Electra Frost Accounting and Tax Services.

nodes.title: Accounting from the Music Industry
nodes.subtitle: Started in 1998 to help musicians not fear tax
nodes.body: Came into accounting from the music industry in 1998. A Sydney accountant took my band's enterprise seriously and his impact was so profound I decided to study to be a public accountant. Spent the next two decades as the accountant who made complex systems navigable for creative people — who would otherwise be excluded from professional advice.

nodes.title: AI Safety as S+G Imperative
nodes.subtitle: Robodebt as the crystallising case
nodes.body: Frontier AI safety is not responsible AI or operational AI governance. It is a social and governance (S+G) accounting imperative. Robodebt crystallised it: automated systems failing people without adequate controls or right of appeal. As more critical infrastructure is rebuilt with AI, someone with real, enforceable duties to the public has to stand between the models and the people they affect. The public accounting profession — obligated to the public interest through IFAC standards of conduct across 130+ jurisdictions — is structurally suited and trusted to provide independent AI governance over AI labs and governments alike. No single lab, state, or institution can legitimately perform that function, or be trusted to distribute it. The public accountant is the missing human regulatory layer.

links.label: The public interest intermediary AI governance is missing
